info.title: Agent Toolbelt
info.description: API microservices for AI agents. Generate schemas, extract structured data from text, and more. Each tool is focused, fast, and billed per call.

get.description: Returns the full catalog of available tools with descriptions, endpoints, and pricing. Use this to discover what tools are available before calling them.

post.summary: Generate a data schema from a description
post.description: Generate JSON Schema, TypeScript interfaces, or Zod validation schemas from a natural language description of a data structure. Useful when you need to create or validate data models on the fly.

description.description: Natural language description of the data structure. Examples: 'a user profile with name, email, and signup date', 'a product listing with title, price, and inventory count', 'an event with start time, end time, and location'.

format.description: Output format. json_schema for standard JSON Schema, typescript for a TS interface, zod for a Zod validation schema.

strict.description: If true, all fields are required. If false, optional fields are marked as optional.

schema.description: The generated schema (object for json_schema, string for typescript/zod)

post.summary: Extract structured data from raw text
post.description: Extract emails, URLs, phone numbers, dates, currencies, addresses, names, or JSON blocks from unstructured text. Useful for parsing documents, emails, web content, or any raw text into structured data.

text.description: The raw text to extract data from. Can be an email body, document content, web page text, chat transcript, etc.

extractors.description: Which types of data to extract from the text. Choose one or more.

deduplicate.description: If true, removes duplicate results within each extractor type.

extracted.description: Extracted data keyed by extractor type. Each value is an array of strings.

post.summary: Convert a schedule description into a cron expression
post.description: Convert natural language schedule descriptions into cron expressions. Returns the expression, human-readable confirmation, field breakdown, and next 5 scheduled run times.

description.description: Natural language schedule description. Examples: 'every weekday at 9am', 'first Monday of each month at noon', 'every 5 minutes', 'every Sunday at 3:30pm'.

post.summary: Build and test a regex from a description
post.description: Build regular expressions from natural language descriptions. Supports 20+ common patterns including emails, URLs, phone numbers, dates, IPs, hex colors, UUIDs, and more. Optionally test against provided strings. Returns pattern, code snippets in JS/Python/TS.

description.description: What to match. Examples: 'email addresses', 'US phone numbers', 'hex color codes', 'semantic version numbers'.

testStrings.description: Optional strings to test the regex against.

post.summary: Normalize a US address to USPS format
post.description: Standardize US mailing addresses to USPS format. Expands abbreviations, corrects capitalization, standardizes street types and directionals, parses components (street number, name, unit, city, state, ZIP), and returns a confidence score.

address.description: The address string to normalize. Examples: '123 main st apt 4b, springfield, il 62701', '456 N. Oak Avenue Suite 200, Los Angeles, CA 90210'.

includeComponents.description: Include parsed address components (streetNumber, streetName, streetType, city, state, ZIP, etc.) in the response.

post.description: Convert CSV data to typed JSON. Supports auto-delimiter detection (comma, semicolon, tab, pipe), header row parsing, type casting (numbers, booleans, nulls), column type inference, and row limits.

delimiter.description: Column delimiter. Use 'auto' to detect automatically.

hasHeader.description: Whether the first row is a header. If false, columns are named col_1, col_2, etc.

typeCast.description: Auto-convert values to numbers, booleans, and nulls where appropriate.

limit.description: Maximum number of rows to return.

skipEmptyRows.description: Skip rows where all values are empty.

post.description: Count tokens for any text across multiple LLM models (GPT-4o, GPT-4, GPT-3.5, Claude 3.x, and more). Returns exact token counts using official tokenizers and estimated API costs per model.

text.description: The text to count tokens for.

models.description: Models to count tokens for. Supported: gpt-3.5-turbo, gpt-4, gpt-4-turbo, gpt-4o, gpt-4o-mini, claude-3-5-sonnet, claude-3-5-haiku, claude-3-opus, claude-3-sonnet, claude-3-haiku, claude-sonnet-4-6, claude-opus-4-6, text-embedding-ada-002, text-embedding-3-small, text-embedding-3-large.

results.description: Token count results keyed by model name.

post.description: Fetch a URL and extract metadata: title, description, Open Graph tags (og:image, og:type, etc.), Twitter card tags, favicon, canonical URL, author, and publish dates. Ideal for enriching links with context.

url.description: The URL to fetch metadata from.

og.description: All og: meta tags as key/value pairs

twitter.description: All twitter: meta tags as key/value pairs

post.description: Convert HTML to clean Markdown or Markdown to HTML. Handles headings, lists, links, code blocks, tables, bold, italic, and more. Ideal for agents that need clean readable content from HTML pages, or need to render Markdown.

headingStyle.description: Heading style for Markdown output: atx uses #, setext uses underlines.

codeBlockStyle.description: Code block style in Markdown output.

post.description: Generate color palettes from a text description or a hex seed color. Returns colors with hex, RGB, and HSL values, WCAG contrast scores against white/black, and ready-to-use CSS custom properties. Supports 35+ aesthetic themes (calm, bold, earthy, neon, pastel, etc.) and complementary/analogous palette generation from a seed hex.

description.description: Text description of the desired palette. Examples: 'calm fintech blue', 'warm earthy tones', 'neon cyberpunk', 'pastel spring'. Use this or seedColor.

seedColor.description: Hex color to build the palette from (e.g. '#3B82F6'). Generates complementary and analogous colors.

includeShades.description: If true, include light and dark shade variants for each color.

post.description: Generate a full brand kit from a company name, industry, and aesthetic keywords. Returns color palette (hex, HSL, RGB with WCAG accessibility scores), typography pairings (curated Google Fonts), and design tokens in JSON, CSS custom properties, or Tailwind config format. Powered by color psychology and professional design principles.

format.description: Output format: full (everything), tokens (JSON), css (custom properties), tailwind (config).

post.description: Compare two versions of any text document and produce a semantic diff. Returns additions, deletions, and modifications with significance ratings. Works with contracts, READMEs, policies, essays, or any text. Powered by Claude.

original.description: The original version of the document.

revised.description: The revised version of the document.

mode.description: 'structured' returns categorized additions/deletions/modifications. 'detailed' returns prose analysis. 'summary' returns a brief overview.

context.description: Optional document type to improve analysis quality (e.g. 'employment contract', 'terms of service').

post.summary: Extract key clauses from a contract
post.description: Extract and summarize key clauses from a contract or legal document. Identifies parties, payment terms, termination conditions, liability, IP ownership, confidentiality, and more. Optionally flags risky or one-sided clauses with severity ratings. Powered by Claude.

contract.description: The contract or legal document text.

clauses.description: Which clause types to extract. Defaults to all.

flagRisks.description: Flag clauses that may be unfavorable or risky.

post.summary: Analyze and improve an LLM prompt
post.description: Score an LLM prompt on clarity, specificity, structure, and completeness, then return an optimized rewrite with a summary of what changed and why. Supports targeting specific models for model-specific best practices.

prompt.description: The LLM prompt to analyze and/or improve.

task.description: What this prompt is trying to accomplish. Helps generate more targeted suggestions.

mode.description: 'both' returns analysis + improved prompt. 'analyze' returns scores and issues only. 'improve' returns rewritten prompt only.

post.summary: Extract action items from meeting notes
post.description: Parse meeting notes or transcripts and extract structured action items with owners, deadlines, and priorities. The 'full' format also returns a summary and list of key decisions. Powered by Claude.

notes.description: Meeting notes or transcript (up to 50,000 characters).

format.description: 'full' returns action items + summary + decisions. 'action_items_only' returns just the task list.

participants.description: Known participant names to improve owner attribution (optional).

200.description: Extracted action items and meeting analysis

post.summary: Strip EXIF/GPS/IPTC/XMP metadata from an image
post.description: Remove all embedded metadata from an image for privacy. Strips EXIF (camera settings, GPS coordinates, timestamps), IPTC (copyright, captions), XMP (editing history), and ICC color profiles. Accepts base64-encoded JPEG, PNG, WebP, or TIFF. Returns a cleaned base64 image with a report of what was removed and the file size reduction.

image.description: Base64-encoded image data (JPEG, PNG, WebP, or TIFF). Do not include the data URI prefix (e.g. 'data:image/jpeg;base64,').

format.description: Output format. 'preserve' keeps the original image format.

quality.description: Output quality for lossy formats (JPEG, WebP). 1-100.

200.description: Cleaned image with metadata removal report

strippedFields.description: Human-readable list of metadata types that were removed

400.description: Invalid image data or unsupported format

BearerAuth.description: API key from Agent Toolbelt. Register at /api/clients/register to get a key. Pass as: Authorization: Bearer atb_...
